title: T-016 — Verify-PIN step-up auth with argon2id (m=64MiB,t=3,p=1) + lockout policy
prompt:
# T-016 — Verify-PIN step-up auth with argon2id (m=64MiB,t=3,p=1) + lockout policy

PRD: `00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md` — sections: §5.x
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`

## Goal
Short-lived PIN for destructive admin actions. Stored as argon2id hash (memory=64MiB, t=3, p=1). Rate limit identical to login.

## Implementation contract
Add packages/auth/src/verify-pin.ts: setPin(userId, pin) hashes via argon2.hash(pin, {type: argon2id, memoryCost: 65536, timeCost: 3, parallelism: 1}). verifyPin(userId, pin) calls argon2.verify and decrements a per-user attempt counter; after 5 failures within 10 minutes the account is soft-locked for 15 minutes (login lockout policy). Persist user_pins(user_id PK, pin_hash, attempts_count INT, locked_until TIMESTAMPTZ). Tests cover correct verify, lockout after 5 wrong attempts, and unlock after 15 minutes via fake timers.

## Files (scope)
- packages/auth/src/verify-pin.ts [create]
- packages/db/migrations/008-pins.sql [create]
- packages/auth/src/__tests__/verify-pin.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given a PIN is set with setPin, when verifyPin is called with the same plaintext, then it returns true and the database stores only the argon2id hash (no plaintext anywhere)
2. Given 5 wrong attempts in 10 minutes, when the 6th attempt is made, then verifyPin returns 'locked' and locked_until is set 15 minutes ahead
3. Given the argon2 parameters, when the hash is decoded, then memory_cost=65536 (64MiB), time_cost=3, parallelism=1

## Test strategy
- RED: write packages/auth/src/__tests__/verify-pin.test.ts covering hash params, lockout, unlock with fake timers before verify-pin.ts exists
- Run 'pnpm --filter @monopilot/auth test'

## Risk red lines
- PIN must NEVER be stored plaintext or with reversible encryption
- Lockout must be enforced server-side; do not rely on client-side counter

## Dependencies (must be complete first)
- T-010

## Closeout evidence
Provide: changed_files, test_commands_and_results, acceptance_criteria_status (per AC), deviations_from_prd, git_status. Use the test-driven-development skill (RED test before implementation) and requesting-code-review at GREEN.
